item.title: White Sox partner faces scrutiny over gender care, drag events and DEI
item.description: Consumers' Research accuses Rush University Medical Center of funding pediatric gender-affirming care and drag brunches while its CEO earns $3.7 million.
item.content:encoded:
When Chicago White Sox fans look past the outfield wall at Guaranteed Rate Field, they see the logo of Rush University Medical Center, the team's official healthcare partner.

What they aren't told at the concession stand is where that health system's cash actually goes: operating a pediatric gender clinic, hosting a "PRIDE Charity Drag Brunch," enforcing mandatory diversity retreats, and paying its top executive nearly $3.7 million in total compensation.

Now, White Sox fans are getting a closer look at the team's healthcare partner.

OPINION: MLB IS FORCING THE GAY AGENDA ON PLAYERS AND FANS, AND WHAT DOES THAT HAVE TO DO WITH BASEBALL?

As fans head to Guaranteed Rate Field for the White Sox-Astros series, Consumers' Research is rolling out a campaign of billboards, street posters, and TV ads targeting the team's healthcare partner.

Consumer watchdog group Consumers' Research launched the "Rush Exposed" campaign, which it exclusively shared with OutKick and Fox News Digital.

Rush signed a multi-year sponsorship as the White Sox's official healthcare partner, paying tens of thousands of dollars for stadium branding, game sponsorships and other marketing.

The Consumers' Research campaign directs fans to RushExposed.com, where Consumers' Research details its allegations against Rush, including its sponsorship of the Chicago White Sox, millions spent on advertising, DEI initiatives, pediatric gender transition services, and "Pride Drag Brunches."

"Rush is betraying what should be every hospital's core mission by using its time, energy, and taxpayer support to promote radical woke activism," Will Hild, executive director of Consumers’ Research, said in a statement.

"Not only does Rush have a history of prioritizing DEI, child sex-change procedures, and climate alarmism, the hospital also has a troubling history of questionable spending on non-medical initiatives completely unrelated to patient care, like its partnership with the Chicago White Sox."

The campaign focuses heavily on Rush’s Affirm Center for Health, a clinic providing so-called gender-affirming services, including mental health support, for minors.

Dr. Loren S. Schechter leads Rush's gender surgery program and is also president-elect of WPATH (World Professional Association for Transgender Health), an organization that publishes standards for transgender healthcare, including previous care for minors.

Rush previously offered gender-affirming care for minors, but in 2025 it announced it has not provided those services for patients under 18 since 2023. 

Rush's internal DEI committee also organized a "PRIDE Charity Drag Brunch," with proceeds benefiting the Affirm Center.

Administrators also required hospital managers to complete "implicit bias" training while pediatric staff at Rush University Children’s Hospital attended DEI retreats covering "microaggressions" and systemic racism.

The campaign also points to what Consumers' Research calls a stark financial contrast.

While Rush recently laid off workers citing financial pressures, public tax filings show CEO Dr. Omar Lateef pulled in nearly $3.7 million in total compensation in FY2024, up from $2.9 million the previous year.

Beyond his salary, the nonprofit also covered Lateef’s housing allowance and membership dues at private organizations like the Economic Club of Chicago.

Even as Rush cut jobs, it continued funding White Sox sponsorships and climate initiatives, including a goal of 100 percent renewable energy by 2030 and vendor policies that weigh race and social impact.

Rush received more than $194 million in federal funding in FY2025 while maintaining its tax-exempt status.

Consumers' Research says Rush became the latest target of its national "Bad Medicine" campaign because the group believes nonprofit hospital systems are using taxpayer support and tax-exempt status to advance political ideology instead of prioritizing patient care.

Rush paid to put its name in front of White Sox fans. Now, Consumers' Research wants those same fans asking what that name stands for.

OutKick reached out to the White Sox and Rush University System for Health for comment.

Send us your thoughts: alejandro.avila@outkick.com / Follow along on X: @alejandroaveela

item.title: Darline Graham reveals what Trump told her inside Oval Office after brother Lindsey's death
item.description: Darline Graham says she used her Oval Office meeting with Trump to bring up Russia sanctions, honoring her late brother Lindsey Graham's Senate legacy.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Darline Graham, R-S.C., revealed what President Donald Trump told her inside the Oval Office just days after the sudden death of her brother, the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, saying the president offered both condolences and encouragement as she stepped into his Senate seat.

"The president was very sincere in his condolences about Lindsey," Graham told Fox News' Sean Hannity on the "Hangout with Sean Hannity" podcast.

"He talked about him and what a good friend he was, how he considered him family."

SEN DARLINE GRAHAM REVEALS WHETHER SHE WILL RUN FOR A FULL TERM DURING 'HANNITY' EXCLUSIVE

Graham, who was appointed by South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster to temporarily fill her brother's seat following his death, said she used the Oval Office meeting to discuss one of the issues she believes was most important to Lindsey: advancing sanctions on Russia.

"I went on to mention that there were some things that I felt like were very important to Lindsey — Russia sanctions — and that I really wanted to push those across the finish line," she said.

Graham was also caught off guard when Trump later publicly urged her to run in the upcoming special election, promising his "complete and total endorsement" if she entered the race.

TRUMP PUSHES LINDSEY GRAHAM'S SISTER TO RUN FOR US SENATE AFTER SHE GETS APPOINTMENT TO FINISH OUT TERM

Nevertheless, she remains determined to keep her brother's legacy alive and has announced plans to run for a full Senate term.

"I feel like I can do this, and the meeting with the president, it was such an honor," she said.

"I told him two things. I said, 'I can tell you this about me: I will work hard, and I'm loyal.'"

Fox News Digital's Nora Moriarty contributed to this report.

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Why Fetterman called Mamdani a 'clown'
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.content:encoded:
Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.

IN TODAY’S NEWSLETTER:
- Fetterman calls Zohran Mamdani a 'clown' over push to arrest Netanyahu in New York City
- IDF says Palestinian soccer players it targeted served Hamas and Jihad terror groups
- Mamdani’s ‘deranged’ Israel take resurfaces as he targets Netanyahu

TOP STORY: Pennsylvania Sen. John Fetterman ripped into NYC’s socialist Mayor Zohran Mamdani, branding him a total "clown" for threatening to arrest Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu in New York. Fetterman slammed Mamdani’s ICC-backed threat as empty posturing, telling the far-left mayor to "sit down" and stick to local issues instead of triggering a wild international circus.

VIDEO: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani branded Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu a "war criminal" and urged the federal government to arrest him under an International Criminal Court warrant. WATCH HERE:

FOUL PLAY: The Israeli Defense Forces slammed wild claims they deliberately targeted innocent Palestinian soccer players, exposing killed athletes, referees, and coaches as covert Hamas and Islamic Jihad terrorists. The IDF revealed the identities of 11 dead players—including three allegedly tied to the brutal October 7 massacre—accusing critics of using sports stars to hide deadly terror ties.

RADICAL RECKONING: A resurfaced clip of NYC’s socialist Mayor Zohran Mamdani calling Israel "an idea" rather than a real country has sparked furious online backlash. Critics blasted the anti-Israel Mayor as "deranged" and out-of-touch, as he continues taking heat for skipping the city's Israel parade and plotting a wild scheme to arrest Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in New York.

MARQUEE MESSAGE: A movie theater outside Portland, Oregon, is facing backlash after it displayed a marquee advertising Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" with a provocative message critics are calling antisemitic. The Lake Theater and Cafe's marquee read, "Before there were the Jews, there was... The Odyssey." The marquee sparked outrage in the Jewish community, and critics claimed the message was historically inaccurate.

GUEST EDITORIAL: Warren Cohn, CEO and founder of Rocketship PR and media adviser to the American Middle East Press Association, writes that Zohran Mamdani's obsession with Israel is coming at the expense of the city he was elected to govern. "New Yorkers elected a mayor to obsess over New York, over crime, over housing, over sanitation, over schools," not a country 6,000 miles away, Cohn writes.

QUOTE OF THE WEEK: "He's just a clown to even say that, so he won't try that. Just sit down and focus on other problems that they have in New York. That's really not your purview. You and I know that. Sit down." - Sen. John Fetterman on New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

- Looking for more on this topic? Find more antisemitism coverage from Fox News here.

- Did someone forward you this email? Subscribe to additional newsletters from Fox News here.

item.title: Joe Rogan says ex-con guest allegedly killed man, hid dismembered remains month after podcast
item.description: Joe Rogan revealed that guest Sheldon Johnson allegedly murdered and dismembered Collin Small in a New York City apartment weeks after the podcast.
item.content:encoded:
Joe Rogan revealed that a former guest on his podcast allegedly murdered someone just a month after he appeared on the podcast.

During Wednesday's episode of "The Joe Rogan Experience," Rogan recalled his 2024 interview with Sheldon Johnson while discussing sociopaths with physician Zach Bush.

Johnson, an ex-con who portrayed himself as a reformed criminal justice advocate, was arrested in spring 2024 for allegedly shooting Collin Small dead in a New York City apartment before dismembering his body and stashing the remains in multiple locations. He was charged with second-degree murder.

Officers who responded to the scene first only discovered Small's headless torso, then located his dismembered feet that were in a plastic bin. His other body parts were allegedly found in Johnson's Harlem apartment.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

"We had a guy in the studio that cut a guy’s head off after the podcast. He killed a guy and cut his head off," Rogan said. "He does the podcast and then afterward goes and visits this guy that apparently he had a beef with, kills him, cuts his head off, puts him in a [suitcase], like puts a wig on, and tries to escape in the hallway through the cameras."

EX-CON NYC MURDER SUSPECT SEEN SMILING WITH DA BRAGG PLEADS NOT GUILTY, HELD WITHOUT BAIL

At the time, Johnson appeared on the podcast with civil rights attorney Josh Dubin, who leads an organization focused on advocating for people who have been wrongly convicted. He was released from prison in 2019 after serving time for a series of armed robberies in 1999.

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

When Johnson appeared on the podcast, he was working as a client advocate for the public defender's office in Queens.

According to the New York Post, a man could be heard begging for mercy before two gunshots rang from the apartment building where Johnson was seen going in and out of in different disguises following the murder.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

Records from the New York Department of Corrections indicated that both Johnson and Small had served time in prison, though the nature of their relationship remained unclear.

During his appearance on Rogan's podcast, Johnson said turning his life around was his goal.

"I said to myself, ‘I’ve been doing bad for so long. I’m going to try to do something good,'" Johnson said.

item.title: Mammoth Red Sox prospect hits home run to the moon, then promptly slaps the catcher and ignites a brawl
item.description: Boston Red Sox No. 3 prospect Justin Gonzales slapped Hub City Spartanburgers catcher Cal Stark in the face after a slow home run trot sparked a brawl.
item.content:encoded:
The Boston Red Sox saw their 15-game winning streak come to an end Wednesday night, but the fire is still BURNING throughout the organization.

And while it may have died down at Fenway with a 5-1 loss to Baltimore, it was still raging in High-A Greenville, where No. 3 prospect Justin Gonzales belted a home run to Virginia, and then promptly ignited a brawl after touching them all.

Are the Sox BACK, or what?!

ZERO BS. JUST DAKICH. TAKE THE DON'T @ ME PODCAST ON THE ROAD. DOWNLOAD NOW!

Let's head down to South Carolina, where Gonzales — who stands at 6-foot-5 and 260 pounds — slapped Hub City Spartanburgers catcher Cal Stark in the face after he said something to Gonzales after a ... slow ... trot around the bases.

Take a look:

Amazing. There is SO much going on there, I don't even know where to begin.

For starters ... what a call by those minor league announcers! I was ready to run through a brick wall. Electric.

Secondly ... how great are minor league team names? The Hub City Spartanburgers? What is that? Nobody knows, but I want a shirt. I need a shirt.

And how about the degrading slap from Gonzales? Not a punch. A slap. That's even worse. It's humiliating. He's such a large human, it's almost impossible not to get in his way. That wing-span is second to none.

Look at how much bigger Gonzales is than everyone else! His teammate, No. 12 (unlisted on the roster), looks like a child trying to hold him back. He stood no chance.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

And then the umpire! Look at the umpire. He attempts to stand in the middle of the two, but quickly retreats. He couldn't abandon ship fast enough. He was outta there.

That's what we call a "business decision," folks. I don't blame him. Fight or flight, and he wasn't gonna win that fight.

Anyway, it's good to see the Sox showing some fight this summer. The big league club just won 15 in a row and put themselves in a playoff race, and it's clearly permeating throughout the organization.

Culture.

item.title: Father turning family tragedy into every baseball fan's dream of visiting 30 MLB stadiums in 30 days
item.description: A Dodgers fan is visiting every MLB stadium in 30 days wearing a life jacket to honor his 21-month-old son who drowned 12 years ago in a pool.
item.content:encoded:
A lifelong baseball fan is living every fan's dream, but unfortunately, it is under tragic circumstances.

Jonathan St. Clair is traveling to each of the 30 MLB stadiums in as many days to spread awareness about water safety, 12 years after losing his 21-month-old son, Jasper, to drowning.

While in the care of a trusted babysitter, Jasper, St. Clair's firstborn, was taken to a home with an ungated pool and left unattended for only minutes. Doctors made every effort to save him for roughly a week, but Jasper unfortunately lost the battle.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

"He had not learned how to swim and had not been to any swim lessons yet. And so, we were very, very taken aback. How come we didn’t know how dangerous water was, or how come we couldn’t put kids in swim lessons earlier?" St. Clair told Fox News Digital in a recent interview.

The interview took place before the sixth game of his trip, a night game in Toronto, after he flew from Denver to watch the Colorado Rockies.

"So, all of those things have transpired, and we’ve learned a whole lot from the community at large. Since then, we have come across not just a family like ours, but hundreds of us who are out there. And really, this campaign, what I’m doing right now, incorporates all of those people. I’m working with organizations just like ours in all 30 cities. The idea is to be able to bring awareness in the water safety and drowning prevention community that it isn’t something that can just happen to someone else. It is something that can happen to you."

Nothing will ever fix that unimaginable burden, but there has been a comfort level in meeting with families who share similar unfortunate experiences, well before this road trip was even a thought.

"I wouldn’t have been able to put one foot forward 12 years ago if it wasn’t for this group of families," St. Clair said.

St. Clair is wearing a Wave Maker Tour life jacket to each game, which initially draws some looks from fellow fans. But when they also spot his MLB cap with all 30 team logos, it becomes easier to strike up a conversation.

"It’s fun to walk around town with a life vest on because everybody, they don’t know what to ask, and then somebody will ask, and it starts the conversation. Actually, it’s been fantastic," St. Clair said.

And as for the hat?

"I feel very in touch with every team that I’m walking into because I’m not coming in as a certain fan. I’m coming in with a cause, and it’s been very well-received. It’s been so much fun."

Already a week into his trip, one father offered up his first-class seat to St. Clair, and another gave him an extra ticket to watch batting practice from behind home plate.

St. Clair, a Los Angeles Dodgers fan from Washington, said he had already been to about 10 or 11 stadiums before this trip, and he knows each one he is yet to attend, his baby should be there.

"Well, I’m a huge baseball fan. I grew up loving baseball. I played baseball. I figured one thing about this sport specifically is it starts with really young kids, as young as 3 usually even start in Little League. My son didn’t get to play the game. I know that he definitely would have," St. Clair said.

"I’ve always wanted to do all 30 baseball stadiums, but I had no concept of doing all of them in 30 days. And, of course, this last year we had a lot of drownings throughout the United States. One thing that I thought, I came up with this harebrained idea: Maybe if I do 30 ballparks in 30 days, we’ll get some recognition and get people talking about drowning prevention."

After catching the Blue Jays lose to the Tampa Bay Rays at Rogers Centre, St. Clair took a flight to Detroit on Wednesday morning to drive to Cleveland for the Guardians' game against the Minnesota Twins. He is driving back to Detroit to watch the Tigers face the Kansas City Royals.

The map is built out – and yes, St. Clair is petrified of rainouts, canceled flights, and just about anything that puts him on a detour of his 17 flights, six drives, four trains, one boat, and one bike (he was ecstatic when he remembered that Rogers Centre has a retractable roof during his rainy day in Canada).

But he likes to think the baseball gods, and his loved ones, are on his side.

"I think even though this whole campaign I’m doing is only 30 days, the bigger goal is after the 30 days, right? What has transpired? What information did we get out there? How much of an actual reach did we make? I’m really hoping that reach was big," St. Clair said.

"I opened up my feed this morning, and five people in Ohio drowned in a river, and that is not what I want to see. I want to wake up in the morning — I know we’re never going to get it to zero — but the goal is to make people aware that if they’re in those rivers and things like that, wear a life jacket. So, we’re working on it one step at a time, and this is it. Thirty days; it’s a pretty tall feat.

"I was on the phone with my wife a couple of hours ago, and she’s like, ‘How are you holding up?’ And I was like, ‘I’m pretty tired today. I’ve got three hours on two flights.’ But it’s OK. I have got so many people behind me, and this isn’t just me. I’m not doing this by myself. I have hundreds of people behind me who have helped coordinate, who are at all of the stops in each place, and they’re making my experience at each stop a real experience, not just a layover flight, so to speak."

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X, and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.

item.title: Saquon Barkley sees 'no reason why' NFL must keep turf fields after FIFA World Cup required grass surfaces
item.description: Saquon Barkley said NFL stadiums installing grass for the World Cup set a precedent, arguing there's no reason NFL players can't have the same surface.
item.content:encoded:
Now that the World Cup has come and gone, NFL players may be more vocal now than ever about their preference to play on grass.

That's largely in part because out of the 11 World Cup venues in the United States, seven of them were NFL stadiums that use artificial turf — but FIFA regulations require a grass playing surface.

The NFLPA has, not so subtly, joked about rolling out the "green carpet" for soccer players this summer, and more recently, players started a social media movement saying that their safety is "#WorthTheCost."

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

But players, including Saquon Barkley, feel that a precedent has been set.

"I think it’s going to be hard for them to argue against that because if you’re able to do it for the World Cup, it doesn’t really make sense," Barkley told Fox News Digital in a recent interview.

Barkley did admit that he is "not as educated on it as I should be" and wants to do some more digging on the injury research between grass and turf, but he knows his own personal preference.

"I do prefer grass. I think the majority of players prefer grass. And I will say this - if the numbers show that playing on grass versus turf is better for us, then I think that’s more than enough," Barkley said.

FORMER NFL STAR SAYS HE 'WOULD DEFINITELY BE' FRUSTRATED WITH NFL LETTING FIFA BRING GRASS TO TURF FIELDS

"There’s no reason why, if you’re able to do it for the World Cup, and if that’s what we’re asking for as players, we can’t do it. I know that’s how players feel. I love playing on grass... Even when you’re practicing on turf, we hate it. We don’t like it. I’ve got to educate myself more on this topic. But if the numbers speak to what I believe they will, then there should be no reason why we shouldn’t."

Ironically, Barkley's serious injuries have come on grass, including a torn ACL in Chicago in 2020, which he admitted put him in a "sticky situation, but there’s a lot of stuff you just can’t control." His 2,000-yard campaign came in his first season with the Philadelphia Eagles, which plays its home games on grass.

Half the league’s stadiums use turf, despite NFLPA Executive Director Lloyd Howell saying that 92% of the league’s players prefer grass. The common speculation, though, is that turf is cheaper to use and easier to maintain than grass, considering the stadiums host plenty more events year-round.

It was announced late last year that each NFL team will be provided with "a library of approved and accredited NFL fields" before the 2026 season begins. Any new field will immediately have to meet those standards, and all teams will have two years to achieve them.

Both grass and synthetic turf fields will be subject to the new standards, but the NFL has no plans to require natural grass fields.

The league’s chief medical officer, Dr. Allen Sills, said there are no "statistically significant differences" in lower-extremity injuries or concussions that can be attributed to the type of playing surface or a specific surface. That is despite widespread preferences by players for grass fields and complaints about surfaces such as the one at MetLife Stadium, where the New York Giants and Jets play and where the World Cup final was held.

Fox News' Chantz Martin contributed to this report.

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X, and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.

item.title: Shane Bieber's strong Rays splits make Toronto Blue Jays the smart first 5 innings MLB bet
item.description: Shane Bieber's recent dominance against Rays hitters and Ian Seymour's vulnerability make the Blue Jays first 5 innings an intriguing MLB bet.
item.content:encoded:
A win can cure basically any frustration you have. The past few days have made me annoyed at how my baseball plays were ending up. It wasn't all bad; I split on Monday, and I won the only MLB play that I had yesterday. Tuesday's looks were not good, and I ran into some bad luck in a few other games that I put out recently. I had a nice, easy 8-0 win on the Washington Nationals yesterday, and I'm hopeful today's game will be another no-sweat contest.

The Tampa Bay Rays are among the best teams in baseball, and I think that most wouldn't have expected this to be the case. It is interesting seeing how this team has gotten it all done, considering they are not loaded with big names, and they never have one of the top payrolls. I always wonder why other teams - both big and smaller market - don't take some of the lessons from them and implement them into their own franchise.

I guess not everyone can replicate everything successfully, but whatever the Rays are doing, they replicate it seemingly year-to-year.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

Today, the Rays are sending out Ian Seymour to the hill. A lot of teams have swingmen in their rotation who bounce between the bullpen and rotation. The Rays typically use "openers" for games, but Seymour seems like he is somewhere between an opener and one they actually want stretched out. He only has seven starts for the season, and you can expect him to throw around five innings and give up three runs.

He has faced the Toronto Blue Jays in a relief role this season, going four games and allowing two earned in four innings of work. Overall, Blue Jays hitters have done well against him, batting 14-for-38.

Toronto is not one of the best teams in baseball. If anything, they are among the most disappointing teams in baseball. After giving the Los Angeles Dodgers everything they could handle last year, expectations were very high. Unfortunately, they haven't come close to those expectations and are 10 games under .500.

I highly doubt they blow up the team, but they may trade away a few pieces. The most glaring issue with the team is Vladimir Guerrero Jr., who is hitting .256 (which isn't bad), but has just six homers for the year. He had nine in 2020, when they played 60 games, and just 15 in 2019, his rookie season, when he played just 123 games.

It isn't as if their pitching has been some shining star, either. They have had trouble on the mound, and that has magnified the offensive issues. Today, they bring in Shane Bieber, who is just 1-1 for the season with a 5.70 ERA and a 1.73 WHIP.

Bieber used to be one of the best arms in the game, and he is still only 31 years old, so I feel like there is more than enough time for him to regain form. He has shown flashes of it as well, including his most recent outing when he went six scoreless innings, allowing three hits, two walks, and striking out six. Rays hitters are batting just .172 against Bieber this season.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

When I look at the total for this game, I want to say the over is the smarter option, but I can make a compelling case for the under.

I likely won't touch the total, but some brief notes on it: Bieber has actually thrown better at home this season, but it is just three starts, so it is a rather small sample size. He only has one game (of five) where he hasn't allowed a run. Seymour is likely to allow some runs if they let him go through the lineup twice. The Rays bullpen is better than Toronto's, so you do have to wonder a bit how long Seymour will go.

For those reasons, I'm avoiding the total. Instead, I want to take the Blue Jays through five innings. I think that Toronto should get a good shot at knocking around Seymour. I also think Bieber has shown good success against the Rays hitters. Give me Toronto through five innings.

For more sports betting information and plays, follow David on X/Twitter: @futureprez2024

item.title: FIRST ON FOX: White House reveals who’s joining Trump at correspondents’ dinner — and who can’t attend
item.description: Trump's Cabinet lineup for the rescheduled White House Correspondents' Association Dinner is revealed, but Melania Trump and JD Vance will not attend.
item.content:encoded:
FIRST ON FOX: President Donald Trump will return to the stage Friday to deliver remarks at the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner, nearly three months after an armed suspect stormed the event in an alleged assassination attempt against him.

The White House exclusively confirmed to Fox News Digital the lineup of administration officials expected to attend the dinner at the Waldorf Astoria in Washington, D.C.

While much of Trump's Cabinet will attend, first lady Melania Trump, Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio will not be there. A White House spokesperson told Fox News Digital their absences are due to prior commitments.

WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENTS' ASSOCIATION PRESIDENT ANNOUNCES EVENT BEING RESCHEDULED AFTER SHOOTING

JOURNALISTS DEMAND WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENTS' ASSOCIATION 'SPEAK TRUTH TO POWER' IN NEW DINNER WITH TRUMP

The banquet will be held at the Waldorf Astoria while the annual dinner has historically been held at the Washington Hilton. Chaos, however, broke out at this year’s banquet when an armed suspect stormed the event in what authorities say was an attempt to assassinate the president.

Weijia Jiang, president of the White House Correspondents’ Association (WHCA), said the event would be a "more intimate gathering" when the new date was announced in June.

Jiang announced the rescheduled date was decided by the WHCA board in an email to association members, sharing the rescheduled event will be a more "intimate gathering."

"This dinner will not only be an opportunity to carry out our program. It will be a statement that violence has no place in American life and a free press will not be intimidated into silence. As you have all demonstrated, courage and community can and should rise above," Jiang added.

item.title: 4 House Republicans rebel against Trump, vote with Dems to block Iran war powers
item.description: House Democrats passed an Iran war powers resolution halting Trump's war in Iran with support from several GOP lawmakers.
item.content:encoded:
House Democrats successfully rebuked President Donald Trump’s war in Iran on Thursday morning, with the help of several Republican lawmakers.

Reps. Jason Crow, D-Colo., and Pramila Jayapal, D-Wash., forced a vote to halt Trump’s military campaign absent congressional approval in a vote of 214-208, with lawmakers largely voting along party lines.

Republicans who supported the Democrat-authored measure included Reps. Warren Davidson, R-Ohio, Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Pa., Tom Barrett, R-Mich., and Thomas Massie, R-Ky.

Massie and Davidson are sharp critics of foreign intervention, while Fitzpatrick and Barrett — two moderate Republicans — are facing competitive re-election bids.

RUBIO SAYS US REMAINS OPEN TO IRAN TALKS BUT WARNS THERE 'WILL BE CONSEQUENCES'

A similar resolution passed the House in June with help from the same four defecting Republicans.

"My position hasn't changed," Barrett, an Army veteran and freshman lawmaker, told Fox News Digital in a statement. "Only Congress has the authority to authorize military action in Iran."

"I remain ready to work with anyone to eliminate hostile threats to our nation without getting us into another endless war," he added.

Thursday's vote is largely symbolic, and the resolution will not go to Trump’s desk for signature if the Senate chamber were also to pass it.

But Democrats' war powers effort underscores that the war in Iran is continuing to divide Republicans, several of whom have complained the White House has not given enough visibility to Congress about the military’s actions.

Just 34% of voters approve of Trump's handling of Iran, according to a Fox News poll released Wednesday. 

Trump and his allies, however, have indicated they are undeterred in preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon.

The vote comes as Trump is reportedly weighing whether to escalate the conflict after Iranian forces killed several U.S. service members in Jordan and Iraq over the weekend. 

U.S. forces completed its 12th consecutive night of strikes on Iran targeting maritime capabilities and air defense assets among other military infrastructure, CENTCOM announced Wednesday.

TRAVELERS UNITE IN POWERFUL MOMENT OF SILENCE AS FALLEN US SERVICE MEMBER RETURNS HOME

"U.S. forces remain vigilant," CENTCOM wrote in a social media post Thursday. 

Democrats forced a vote on a war powers resolution aimed at disapproving the Middle East conflict using a mechanism called a privileged resolution, which allows rank-and-file lawmakers to move legislation over the opposition of House GOP leadership. 

Crow, an Army veteran, said in a statement, "18 American servicemembers have been killed since this war began. Hundreds more have been injured. Billions of taxpayer dollars have been spent. The President’s war with Iran has been an unmitigated disaster."

"The costs are rising every day, yet the President is choosing to double down on his failures," he continued.

The vote is one of several instances this year of House Republicans rebuking Trump and his policies, a symbol of the razor-thin majorities the GOP holds in Congress.

House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, D-N.Y., took a victory lap following the successful war powers vote Thursday.

"Donald Trump and Republicans have failed the American people, which is why they clearly are on the run and are on the verge of losing control of the House of Representatives," he said during a news conference.

Trump ripped the four GOP defectors following the successful war powers vote in June, calling them "unpatriotic."

item.title: Hasan Piker makes AOC his 2028 pick for president, says it's 'not even a question’
item.description: Hasan Piker chose AOC over Newsom, Buttigieg, and other Democrats in a 2028 presidential exercise, calling her even better than Bernie Sanders.
item.content:encoded:
Far-left Twitch streamer Hasan Piker selected Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., over a slate of prominent Democrats as his preferred 2028 presidential candidate on Wednesday — arguing that AOC could excite the Democratic Party's base and bring infrequent voters into a winning coalition.

When asked to choose between Ocasio-Cortez and Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., as his preferred candidate, Piker immediately selected the New York congresswoman.

"AOC. It's not even a question," Piker said during a Zeteo interview with Mehdi Hasan.

KAMALA HARRIS IS THE 'FAIRLY OBVIOUS' 2028 PRESIDENTIAL FRONTRUNNER, DEMOCRATIC INSIDERS SAY

Piker made the choice at the end of a rapid-fire exercise that began with California Gov. Gavin Newsom and former Chicago Mayor Rahm Emanuel. The list later included former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg, former Vice President Kamala Harris, Sen. Jon Ossoff, D-Ga., Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker, Sen. Chris Van Hollen, D-Md., and Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif.

Piker said Ocasio-Cortez stood apart from the other potential contenders presented to him.

"AOC is, out of all of the people that you mentioned," Piker said. "AOC is the only one that I would like."

Piker acknowledged that her race and gender could present electoral obstacles but rejected the argument that they would make victory impossible.

WATCH: AOC WON'T RULE OUT SENATE BID AFTER NEW YORK PROGRESSIVES NOTCH PRIMARY WINS: 'INSPIRED AND ENCOURAGED'

"These are hurdles, yes," Piker said. "But these are hurdles that one can clear."

Piker argued Ocasio-Cortez could overcome those barriers by campaigning with conviction and reaching voters who do not regularly participate in elections.

"As long as you have moral conviction, as long as you can excite the base, as long as you can actually create a unique coalition and activate low-propensity voters," Piker said. "Which I think AOC has the capacity to do, it would be a fantastic left candidate."

SOCIALISM’S RISE INSIDE THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY NOW THREATENS THE AMERICAN DREAM

He compared Ocasio-Cortez favorably with Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., whose presidential campaign helped elevate democratic socialism within the party.

"It would be a fantastic left candidate, even better than Bernie in many ways," Piker said.

Piker also offered criticism of Ocasio-Cortez's political presentation. He said she had been effective on social media and willing to take progressive positions in Congress but argued she could appear too concerned with optics.

"The way she carries herself, I think, is a little bit too, like, optics-driven, too liberal and too optics-driven," Piker said. "I think she doesn’t harness the populist rage in the way that she communicates."

Fox News Digital reached out to Ocasio-Cortez for comment but did not immediately hear back.

Ocasio-Cortez has not announced a 2028 campaign and said in May that she had not decided whether to run for president. She had increased her national travel, endorsements and campaign appearances, including a recent Michigan stop for progressive Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed.

item.title: Surveillance video shows officers tackle 'anti-American' suspect after Federal Building firebombing
item.description: Surveillance video shows the moment Andrew Arrabaca allegedly fired fireworks and dumped gasoline outside 26 Federal Plaza in New York City.
item.content:encoded:
NEW YORK — Federal prosecutors have released new surveillance video of an alleged arson attack on a government building in New York City that houses offices for the FBI, ICE and more.

Andrew Arrabaca, a 43-year-old man originally from Poughkeepsie, New York, is accused of launching fireworks at 26 Federal Plaza in Manhattan before dumping gasoline on the steps outside and lighting it on fire, according to authorities.

The video shows a man alleged to be him in camouflage pants and a helmet, pulling a cart with an anti-ICE banner hanging from the front.

ARMY VETERAN FACES ARSON CHARGE AFTER ATTACK OUTSIDE NYC FEDERAL BUILDING: 'CAME ARMED WITH BAD INTENTIONS'

The suspect can be seen placing fireworks on the ground before firing them toward pedestrians outside. He reenters the frame with a BB or pellet gun and begins shooting before dumping a can of gasoline on the steps and lighting them on fire.

Before the video ends, the figure can be seen throwing a box into the flames, which investigators later said contained more fireworks.

Federal officers arrived and arrested him at the scene.

VIDEO SHOWS MOMENT CHICAGO MAN ALLEGEDLY USED SUV AS WEAPON AGAINST ICE AGENT, PROSECUTORS SAY

At least one person was injured by explosive debris, suffering from a gash in the back of their head, according to court filings. Two other people suffered minor injuries.

Arrabaca is accused of carrying a slew of additional weapons and incendiary devices. He was initially accused of firing an Airsoft rifle, but prosecutors later said it was a BB or pellet gun, and photos show at least two on the ground after his arrest.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

SEND US A TIP

"He is an anti-American, anti-government extremist," James Barnacle, the assistant director in charge of the FBI New York Field Office, told reporters at a news briefing Monday. "We know that from what he said." He added that Arrabaca allegedly "came armed, with bad intentions."

FORMER MARINE GETS 100 YEARS IN PRISON AS JUDGE CALLS ICE DETENTION CENTER AMBUSH 'AN ASSAULT ON DEMOCRACY'

According to the criminal complaint, Arrabaca allegedly told investigators he had been "motivated to act by anti-government beliefs, including a belief that government authorities were unlawfully harming people" and that he targeted the staircase because he knew it led to a busy entrance.

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

Barnacle said Arrabaca is a former U.S. Army soldier who served from 2001 to 2005 as a missile system mechanic. Voter records show he has registered as a Democrat and a member of the left-wing Working Families party.

Three people suffered minor injuries in the attack. "Mr. Arrabaca was looking to injure people and harm people," Barnacle said. "He had two axes, a hammer, a machete and three knives."

He added that police also recovered six more fireworks, "some other destructive items" as well as anti-ICE literature. Authorities seized Arrabaca's vehicle and were working to obtain a search warrant as Barnacle delivered his update Monday.

Arrabaca does not appear to have a criminal record prior to his arrest outside the building.

The Federal Building has long been a target for extremists, Barnacle said. It's also one of the most secure locations in Manhattan, surrounded by barricades and road closures.

item.title: DAVID MARCUS: In Middle America, socialist politics ages like milk
item.description: Socialist candidates like Melat Kiros and Darializa Chevalier are winning primaries using a last-minute surprise model that blindsides voters.
item.content:encoded:
Erick was here for a conference on transportation technology when I talked to him about his hometown of Denver, and how socialist congressional candidate Melat Kiros recently won a shocking Democratic primary over her more moderate rival.

"I’m a Democrat," Erick told me. "I lean a bit to the Republican side maybe, but these socialists are crazy."

In his 40s, having lived for 15 years in England, Erick is socially liberal, but also patriotic. He has concerns about AI and data centers, but also sees their utility. In short, he is exactly the kind of Democratic voter that is thinking twice about his affiliation with the party amid the rise of Marxism within.

I asked how long before Election Day Erick had even heard of Kiros and her commie candidacy.

"A week, maybe?" he said. "It was fast, and I’m even involved in the very local politics on the homeowner level. She just came out of nowhere."

This is a repeating pattern now for the far-left socialist congressional candidates across the country. Nobody in New York, or beyond, knew who Darializa Chevalier was about two weeks before she knocked off longtime Rep. Adriano Espailat. Same for likely future member of Congress Claire Valdez.

This is the same model that was used by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani in his 2025 primary race, and by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez in 2018 when she knocked off Rep. Joe Crowley. It has three steps.

First, a 24/7, 365-day-a-year organizing machine that harnesses and harvests votes for the socialist party. Second, the use of non-traditional media to introduce the wacky candidate to the equally wacky activist class. Third, a mainstream introduction with very little time left to kick the socialist tires.

This is exactly how, by the time Erick had ever heard of Milot Kiros, she had all but already won.

As successful as this socialist model has been in congressional races across the nation, Senate contests are proving to be a tougher nut to crack, in large part because they require candidates like Adbul El-Sayed in Michigan to stay in the spotlight for an extended period. That would be the same spotlight that melted former Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner.

It brings to mind the presidential candidacy of Kamala Harris, which she says she lost because 107 days wasn’t enough time to campaign. In fact, it was probably too long to campaign; she might have won a 15-day race, and that is exactly the type the DSA likes to run.

As Terry, who is in her 30s and a Detroit native, told me of El-Sayed, "There keep being these crazy things he said in the past, they keep coming," a fact that even CNN’s Jake Tapper acknowledged in an interview with El-Sayed about his refusal to celebrate the death of Iran’s ayatollah, which went so badly for the candidate that El-Sayed is now trashing CNN at his rallies.

The biggest hope now for El-Sayed’s opponent Rep. Haley Stevens is that he has peaked too soon. Her ads are hitting the airwaves at a frantic pace in an effort to make that so.

When I arrived in Detroit last week, there were still barely any TV ads airing. By the day I left, Stevens ads were thick on screens everywhere, news channels, sports channels, the Hallmark channel, you name it.

The theme of these ads, beyond just saying that El-Sayed is not electable, are his previous comments attacking both former President Barack Obama and current Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, neither of whom have endorsed in the race.

The message is clear: El-Sayed is not a normal Democrat, but a radical, and Stevens still has two weeks left to make this charge stick as Abdul insists he isn’t a socialist while sharing the stage with Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., and AOC.

Unlike Erick in Colorado, who had the sudden success of socialist Kiros thrust upon him before he even knew it was happening, Michigan voters have had time to examine El-Sayed and still have time to do so.

It raises an important question for our current political moment: Are the Marxists of the Democratic Socialists of America even trying to win the votes of Democrats like Erick, or are they simply trying to blindside them?

The sneaky, last-minute surprise approach has done well for the communists on the congressional level, but if they want to win statewide, or nationwide, they need to win American voters over, not simply pull a fast one on them.

CLICK HERE TO READ MORE FROM DAVID MARCUS

item.title: Shania Twain calls Ella Langley an 'authentic songwriter' with a unique sound in new interview
item.description: Shania Twain calls Ella Langley an authentic songwriter with a unique sound in a new Rolling Stone interview, praising her unapologetic persona.
item.content:encoded:
Shania Twain has extremely high praise for Ella Langley.

As our readers are aware, Langley has been experiencing a meteoric rise in the music industry that has been nothing short of insane to watch.

The "Nicotine" and "Dandelion" singer is the undisputed face of women in country music. She dominates the charts, racks up awards and has built a following so loyal that it's borderline hard to believe.

COUNTRY SUPERSTAR ELLA LANGLEY PULLS OFF INCREDIBLE CAREER ACCOMPLISHMENT ON THE MUSIC CHARTS

Now, one of the true female trailblazers in country music is pouring a ton of praise on Langley.

Shania Twain was an unstoppable force in country music going from breaking through in the 1990s. She paved the way for a lot of acts who would follow, and to put it as simply as possible, she's the definition of a legend.

What does she think about the woman who appears to be following a similar path?

SHANIA TWAIN SAYS SHE'S FINALLY 'GETTING IT RIGHT' IN HER 60S AFTER STARTING LIFE ‘ON THE TOUGH SIDE’

She's a big fan, and thinks the reasons for Langley's success are fairly straightforward and simple.

Twain said the following in an interview with Rolling Stone that was released on Wednesday:

ELLA LANGLEY GOES VIRAL WITH PASSIONATE COMMENTS ABOUT HER CHRISTIAN FAITH, GOD, LOVE AND RELIGION

Imagine how fast the internet and country music world would break if Langley and Shania Twain teamed up for some kind of collaboration.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

It would put up generational numbers and go viral so fast that your head would be spinning. I don't want to take credit for floating the idea if it actually happens, but bookmark this just in case it does.

It makes too much sense to ignore.

It's also great to see Twain share some positivity in this world. We could all use more of that. Let me know your thoughts at David.Hookstead@outkick.com.

item.title: Lindsey Graham's rise from family tragedy to political power explored in Fox Nation special
item.description: A brand new Fox Nation special traces Lindsey Graham's path from rural South Carolina to the U.S. Senate through personal tragedy and resilience.
item.content:encoded:
Long before becoming one of the most influential voices in American politics, Lindsey Graham overcame extraordinary personal hardship on a journey that would take him from rural South Carolina to the U.S. Senate.

"They say Abe Lincoln was our log cabin president. I guess Lindsey was our mobile home senator," Graham's law school roommate Warren Mowry said in the new Fox Nation special, "The Life and Legacy of Lindsey Graham."

Through video clips and interviews from colleagues, friends and former rivals, the special traces Graham's life from his modest upbringing and early family tragedy of losing his parents to his decades of public service — offering an intimate look at the experiences that shaped one of the Republican Party's most influential lawmakers.

MAJORITY LEADER THUNE: LINDSEY GRAHAM WAS THE GOP’S GLUE, BINDING US TOGETHER WITH COURAGE AND GRACE

"Lindsey honestly is an American success story when you get down to it, because he came from literally the most humble of beginnings," said Kevin Bishop, Graham's former communications director.

The special explores how adversity shaped Graham's sense of responsibility, particularly after he became the legal guardian of his younger sister, Darline Graham Nordone, when their parents died just 15 months apart.

"He became the caretaker, the guardian, in order that she wouldn't be declared an orphan and taken into foster care," U.S. Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee said, reflecting on Graham's actions.

WHO IS DARLINE GRAHAM NORDONE? LINDSEY GRAHAM'S SISTER EMERGES AS TRUMP'S SENATE PICK

Bishop separately discussed how Graham became "everything" Darline needed him to be, stepping up to the plate to become "mom, dad... sounding board" and more.

"Darline will tell you that, and that's why they were so close," he said. "They were incredibly close, and Lindsey loved her to death."

Darline stepped up to the plate in her own way following her brother's death earlier this month, answering calls from President Donald Trump – and South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster – when asked to fill her brother's Senate seat for the remainder of his term.

SEN DARLINE GRAHAM REVEALS WHETHER SHE WILL RUN FOR A FULL-TERM DURING ‘HANNITY’ EXCLUSIVE

Citing her brother's commitment to serving the people of South Carolina, Darline has since announced she will seek a full Senate term, saying she hopes to carry on the legacy he spent a lifetime building.

"The Life and Legacy of Lindsey Graham" is now streaming exclusively on Fox Nation.

CLICK HERE TO JOIN FOX NATION

item.title: Bodycam shows Connecticut officer repeatedly ordering bat-wielding suspect to stop before fatal shooting
item.description: Body camera footage shows a Meriden police officer ordering Robert Lee Jenkins III to drop a baseball bat before firing as the suspect bludgeoned a victim.
item.content:encoded:
A Connecticut police officer repeatedly ordered a man to drop a baseball bat before opening fire as the suspect continued bludgeoning a motionless man in the head, newly released body camera footage shows.

The footage, released this week by the Connecticut Office of Inspector General, captures the July 19 encounter in Meriden that left two men dead, including 38-year-old Robert Lee Jenkins III, who investigators said was shot by a responding officer while carrying out the attack.

According to the inspector general's preliminary report, Meriden Officer Anthony Offiaeli arrived at a home on Sagamore Road shortly after 10 a.m. and found Jenkins "bludgeoning" a man lying motionless on the front lawn with a baseball bat.

VIDEO SHOWS UNDERCOVER COPS SHOOTING MAN THREATENING SHOPPERS WITH FAKE GUN IN ‘JUSTIFIED’ DEFENSE: OFFICIALS

"Put the f---ing bat down!" the officer shouted repeatedly while pointing his firearm at Jenkins, body camera footage shows.

He then radioed for backup, urging responding officers to "step it up."

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

As Offiaeli advanced, Jenkins swung the baseball bat downward four times, appearing to strike the victim on the ground with each blow.

The officer fired multiple shots, but Jenkins continued striking the victim with the bat. The officer fired additional rounds before Jenkins began to slow down, though he continued swinging the bat until he eventually collapsed.

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE

The inspector general's preliminary report states Offiaeli ordered Jenkins to drop the baseball bat four times before opening fire after Jenkins continued striking the victim.

Jenkins received medical treatment at the scene before being taken to Hartford Hospital, where he died at 11:30 p.m., investigators said.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

The victim who was attacked with the baseball bat was pronounced dead at the scene. His identity has not been released because Connecticut law prohibits disclosing the identity of victims in family violence cases.

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

An autopsy determined Jenkins died from multiple gunshot wounds to the torso, and the manner of death was ruled a homicide, according to the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner.

GOT A TIP?

The victim's cause of death was determined to be blunt force head injuries, and the manner of death was also ruled a homicide. Toxicology results and Jenkins' death certificate were still pending as of Tuesday.

GET BREAKING NEWS BY EMAIL

In an initial statement following the shooting, Meriden Police Chief Roberto Rosado said the incident "reminds us that the dangers of Police work is real" and that there was no ongoing threat to the public.

The Connecticut Office of Inspector General, Connecticut State Police Central District Major Crime Squad, the Meriden Police Department and the New Haven Judicial District State's Attorney's Office are continuing to investigate the incident.

item.title: The hidden left-wing network powering a ‘conservative’ rebellion against a major Trump priority
item.description: Humans First enlisted Green Party figures and Democratic-aligned activists despite billing itself as a conservative grassroots movement.
item.content:encoded:
A group billing itself as a grassroots conservative movement against new data centers carried out a series of nationwide protests enlisting the help of Green Party figures and Democratic-aligned activists, a Fox News Digital review found.

Though initially launched as a bipartisan project, Humans First has since become — in its own words — a "conservative social movement that is dedicated to ensuring that the future of AI is in the hands of everyday people." Humans First's nascent movement was supposed to build up to mass protests on July 18, when the organization says it sought to rally conservatives against the construction of new data centers.

The push put Humans First in tension with one of President Donald Trump’s major economic priorities — accelerating the data-center buildout needed to power America’s AI race.

TOP ENERGY GROUP WARNS WEALTHY FOREIGNERS ARE POTENTIALLY BANKROLLING ANTI-DATA CENTER CAMPAIGNS ACROSS US

While right-wing activist Amy Kremer is the public face of Humans First, the group’s paper trail shows that the organization didn’t start with her or even another conservative. Incorporation records reviewed by Fox News Digital list staffers from the Center for AI Safety, a San Francisco-based nonprofit, as Humans First’s initial employees. The Center for AI Safety also provided a loan to Humans First to serve as start-up capital, NBC News previously reported.

In a similar vein, several of the local organizers named on Humans First’s website as planning the July 18 protests had backgrounds in left-of-center politics.

"I was one of the earliest leaders of the Tea Party movement in 2009, and I can tell you that the disconnect between the elites and the base that gave rise to the Tea Party movement can be seen today in the battle over AI data centers," Kremer, chair of Humans First, told Axios in June.

During the Obama administration, Kremer was a top Tea Party activist and, more recently, she was one of the organizers of the "Save America" rally that preceded the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol — a role that later became a focal point during her unsuccessful 2022 run for a Georgia congressional seat.

Her group says the July 18 protests were meant to "give grassroots Americans, particularly grassroots conservatives, a voice in the critical debate over policies relating to the building of massive AI data centers."

Despite Kremer’s GOP bona fides and her stated goal of mobilizing conservatives, Fox News Digital found that several listed protest organizers had left-wing, Green Party or Democratic-aligned backgrounds, including a transgender Green Party House candidate, local progressive activists and a Democratic state legislative candidate.

FIRST ON FOX: POWERFUL HOUSE WAYS AND MEANS CHAIR THROWS HAMMER DOWN ON 'FOREIGN-ALIGNED INFLUENCE NETWORK'

At the bottom of the Humans First webpage advertising the protests, the organization stated that the events it is promoting have not "necessarily" been "fully vetted or endorsed by Humans First."

Humans First’s website listed a chapter of the Illinois Green Party as organizers for a protest just outside Chicago.

Among other causes, the Illinois Green Party supports solidarity with Cuba, divesting from Israel, expanding abortion access, decriminalizing prostitution, restricting gun laws and impeaching Trump.

Also affiliated with the Illinois Green Party is Alexandria Keating-Sofiakis, a transgender activist and congressional candidate who organized a separate Humans First protest about 50 miles northwest of Chicago. 

Kremer has repeatedly posted critical social media messages about transgender issues, Fox News Digital found.

Keating-Sofiakis’ campaign website lists support for the Green New Deal, expanded abortion rights, ending American support for Israel and "Medicare-for-all." Though the two disagree on much, Keating-Sofiakis told Fox News Digital that opposition to data centers transcends traditional ideological boundaries and made working with Kremer feasible.

"Data centers are a cross-partisan problem," Keating-Sofiakis said. "From a conservative worldview, the drop in property values, increase in water costs, as well as increase in electrical costs that are affiliated with data centers are all antithetical to conservative values. Likewise, from a progressive worldview, the ecological damage alone associated with data centers is more than enough justification to oppose them."

Keating-Sofiakis also pointed out that local Green Party activists received support from prominent conservatives such as Tomi Lahren and Lara Trump in a recent effort to free dogs from a medical facility.

"I am no longer hesitant to work with conservative figures and organizations on issues we share in common," Keating-Sofiakis told Fox News Digital.

600 GROUPS WITH $2B IN REVENUE MOBILIZE 3,000 MAY DAY PROTESTS IN A 'RED-BLUE' ALLIANCE, PROBE FINDS

Humans First defended its conservative credentials when reached by Fox News Digital.

"First off, we are happy to put our grassroots conservative and America First credentials up against anyone. I was one of the founders of the Tea Party movement back in 2009, and I was one of the earliest and most high profile Trump supporters back in 2015," Kremer told Fox News Digital. "The men and women on our team are longtime conservative activists who have dedicated their lives to electing conservatives and fighting for conservative policies."

Most of the official staff listed on Humans First’s website have spent a significant portion of their careers working in conservative politics.

"This line of attack is preposterous but not surprising. This is exactly the same line of attack we saw during the early days of the Tea Party. The establishment and their handmaidens in the media were willing to smear Tea Party activists as racists and bigots in their effort to defend the big interests of their corporate masters," Kremer continued. "Instead of questioning our conservative credentials, how about asking how any so-called conservative can take money from the same big tech and big AI billionaires who have worked tirelessly to destroy the conservative movement over the last 10 years."

Fox News Digital obtained footage of five Humans First protests across California, Georgia, Missouri and South Carolina. Multiple demonstrators at the event in Gilroy, California, had signs protesting Immigration and Customs Enforcement — a common antagonist for those on the left. Some of the protests were sparsely attended.

Nearly 1,000 miles south of its Green Party-linked protests in Illinois, Humans First’s protest page listed a Georgia activist group called People for Progress as the organizer of a local demonstration. The group was founded after the 2024 election to resist Trump’s agenda and protect "diversity, equity & justice."

In Missouri, Humans First’s protest page listed the founder of CleanEarth4Kids as an organizer for a Springfield protest. CleanEarth4Kids has publicly urged followers to oppose several Republican-backed measures and support Democratic-backed bills. Ferguson Advocates for Civic Transparency (FACT), another group run by left-of-center individuals, organized an additional Humans First demonstration just outside of St. Louis.

MARXIST ORGANIZER LEADS CAMPAIGN AGAINST VIRGINIA AI DATA CENTER AS FOREIGN INFLUENCE CONCERNS MOUNT

A press release obtained by Fox News Digital shows that the featured speakers at the event included a former Democratic state lawmaker, a current Democratic candidate for the state legislature and a member of the Party for Socialism and Liberation.

Lady Huston, one of the organizers leading FACT, told Fox News Digital that Humans First hosted weekly Zoom meetings for its organizers and sent out regular mass emails with directions and suggestions for them. She, like the other left-of-center organizers working with Humans First, also stressed the opposition to data centers should be a nonpartisan issue.

"[Sen. Josh Hawley] is making statements and saying we need to do something about [data centers]. So, I never thought I'd care about anything this man had to say. But he is in tune with what's happening with AI… and that we need some better understanding about the effects of data centers," Huston told Fox News Digital. "So, I'm with him on that. You know, so it doesn't matter to me what political party you are, but the subject is what matters."

Back north in Michigan, Humans First listed Mitchell Distin, a Democrat running for state House, as an organizer for a protest, but has since removed that demonstration from its webpage. Fox News Digital reviewed a prior version of the page listing Distin before the demonstration was removed.

"While you are correct in your assessment that I am, by no means, conservative, the data center issue isn't a left-right issue," Distin told Fox News Digital. "It's an issue without precedent, as it transcends party boundaries. And for that reason alone, I believe it's showing that every day, common Americans actually have far more in common than they realize. We actually agree on the futures we want, not the futures that Big Tech execs want for us."

TOP ENERGY GROUP WARNS WEALTHY FOREIGNERS ARE POTENTIALLY BANKROLLING ANTI-DATA CENTER CAMPAIGNS ACROSS US

Not all the organizers who have signed up to facilitate Humans First’s nationwide protests are political liberals.

Cathy Latham, for example, is a longtime Georgia GOP official who is now working as a staffer with Humans First. She organized a July 18 protest in Waco, Texas. Latham was among the 18 defendants, including Trump, indicted in Georgia’s 2020 election-interference case.

Humans First claimed after July 18 that it had helped organize 142 protests across 42 states.

Kremer, speaking to the Washington Examiner in May, expressed disgust with how AI companies have spent millions on lobbying and messaging campaigns to influence policy.

"I’m not for sale," she told the Washington Examiner. "When I do something, I pour my whole heart and soul into it."

item.title: Kyrie Irving, Victor Wembanyama like Mamdani's video calling Netanyahu a 'war criminal'
item.description: Dallas Mavericks guard Kyrie Irving and Spurs star Victor Wembanyama face backlash after liking Zohran Mamdani's Instagram video on Netanyahu.
item.content:encoded:
Dallas Mavericks guard Kyrie Irving and San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama signaled support for New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's latest anti-Israel message by liking an Instagram video in which he called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu a "war criminal" and urged federal authorities to arrest him if he enters New York City.

The video marked one of Mamdani's strongest public attacks on Israel since taking office.

In the clip, Mamdani accused Netanyahu of committing genocide against Palestinians and called on the federal government to execute the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant if the Israeli leader visits New York City.

MAMDANI SETS INTERNET ABLAZE WITH VIDEO EXPLAINING CAVE ON PLAN TO ARREST NETANYAHU: 'WHAT A JOKE'

Mamdani's remarks made no mention of Hamas' Oct. 7 terrorist attacks on Israel.

Wembanyama (the league's next "face") and Irving appeared to endorse rhetoric condemned as virulent, reckless and inherently antisemitic.

Mamdani conceded New York City couldn't arrest Netanyahu, then called on the federal government to do it instead.

"Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people," Mamdani declared.

He went on to accuse Netanyahu of responsibility for civilian deaths and humanitarian conditions in Gaza.

"He is responsible for the killing of more than 73,000 people, for the maiming of tens of thousands of children as those who survive undergo amputation without anesthesia, for the targeting of neonatal hospitals and maternity care centers, denying newborns even the chance to live," Mamdani claimed.

Mamdani also accused Israel of blocking humanitarian aid, gunning down aid workers and journalists, and deliberately targeting Palestinian children while arguing that American taxpayers continue to fund the war.

NETANYAHU FIRES BACK AT NYC MAYOR MAMDANI'S VOW TO ENFORCE 'BOGUS ARREST WARRANT'

"There is a reason the International Criminal Court issued a warrant for his arrest," Mamdani said.

"As I've said, I agree with the ICC that Benjamin Netanyahu should be arrested and tried for his crimes, as I do for anyone else charged by the ICC."

"Benjamin Netanyahu is not welcome in New York City, nor is any other war criminal at large," Mamdani stated.

Jewish advocacy groups have argued Mamdani's antisemitic sentiments contribute to rising antisemitism while singling out Israel.

For Irving, the like reopens a chapter about past antisemitism controversies many believed had been put behind him.

In 2022, while with the Brooklyn Nets, Irving was suspended at least five games after promoting the film "Hebrews to Negroes: Wake Up Black America," which contained antisemitic material, including Holocaust denial.

He later apologized, met with Anti-Defamation League leaders and Jewish community representatives, and completed a series of conditions set by the team before returning to the court.

The Nets traded Irving to Dallas in 2023, where he has largely stayed out of similar controversies until now.

OutKick reached out to representatives for both Irving and Wembanyama seeking comment on their Instagram activity. Neither responded before publication.

Send us your thoughts: alejandro.avila@outkick.com / Follow along on X: @alejandroaveela

item.title: Joy Behar shocks 'The View' hosts, audience after on-air kiss with guest Anson Mount
item.description: Joy Behar stunned the audience by kissing "Star Trek" actor Anson Mount on the lips, later revealing on the podcast what prompted the spontaneous moment.
item.content:encoded:
Joy Behar shocked her fellow "The View" co-hosts and the audience when she kissed actor Anson Mount during Wednesday's show.

Co-host Whoopi Goldberg introduced Mount and the actor started walking towards "The View" table and greeted the hosts. When he greeted Behar, the co-host ended up kissing him on the lips.

Co-host Sunny Hostin pointed at the pair and the other co-hosts laughed.

CONSERVATIVE WATCHDOG TELLS FCC THAT 'THE VIEW' IS A DEMOCRATIC PARTY OPERATION, NOT A NEWS PROGRAM

After Mount sat down, Behar said, "You know something? I'm old, but I'm not dead."

"That's good, Joy, because I am," Mount said.

Behar said the last time Mount visited with "The View," he brought her flowers for her birthday and that it had never been the same between them.

JD VANCE WINS GRUDGING PRAISE FROM JOY BEHAR ON 'THE VIEW,' WHO TELLS HIM TO RUN FOR PRESIDENT

"Well, I got married," he said.

Behar addressed the kiss during the "Behind the Table" podcast on Wednesday, telling "The View" producer Brian Teta, "Well, all morning I've been hearing about Anson Mount, and I've been making the jokes about his name."

"And then when he came out I just felt like kissing him," Behar said.

Teta asked Behar if she had heard from her husband, Steve Janowitz. The co-host said he texted her during the show and said, "Cut that out."

Mount spoke to the co-hosts about his "Star Trek" series, "Strange New Worlds."

CLICK HERE FOR MORE COVERAGE OF MEDIA AND CULTURE

"It was my make-believe game when I was a child, there’s not a day that’s gone by I haven’t looked around and said to myself, ‘I’m from White Bluff, Tennessee. How did I get to be in the captain’s chair?’" he said during the interview.

During the show on Wednesday, the co-hosts also discussed hearings regarding the Smithsonian Institution on Capitol Hill.

Hostin argued during the discussion that Republicans were targeting the Smithsonian because the Trump administration didn't want to pay reparations to Black Americans.

"Reparations," Hostin said. "I think this reflects a broad effort by the Trump administration to reshape the way the nation’s history is told, so this country does not have to pay Black people for the forced labor and the unpaid labor that built this country. I really think that this is what this is about."

"Let’s face it, when you have truth and reconciliation commissions, and you've had them when it comes to interned Japanese Americans. You've had them when it comes to the Holocaust. You’ve had them when it comes to Native Americans," she continued. "It has not happened — truth and reconciliation commissions — when it comes to slavery. And that is because they’re trying to bury the truth part. You cannot have true reconciliation, and then you cannot have true financial repair unless you have the truth. And they’re trying to erase that truth."

Hostin concluded, "It’s about this country trying to avoid paying African Americans our due."

item.title: Maria Sharapova attacks the dog days of summer by hopping into the pool, it pays to be OSU's QB & Shatner
item.description: Miami University's Paulina Adams revealed she's dating Ohio State quarterback Julian Sayin, officially kicking off the college football season.
item.content:encoded:
The sun is out, it's 59 brisk degrees here in Ohio and it's time to fire off a fresh edition of Thursday Screencaps where I better not hear anyone north of the Ohio River complaining this morning. We're heading for a high of 80. I am talking one of the most beautiful July days in the history of this state.

Speaking of Ohio, my home state, where I was born, where my kids were born, where I went to college, let's head down state route 23 to Columbus where our Ohio State quarterback seems to be having himself quite a summer.

Miami University sorority girl and pilates instructor Paulina Adams — I'd never heard of her — revealed she's dating Julian Sayin. And just like that, the college football season is officially off and running for this column. For Screencaps, this is like a cannon going off at a golf course to start a golf scramble (Green Hills golf course in Clyde, OH fires off a cannon; it's great).

CHRISTIAN PULISIC RECOVERS FROM HIS WORLD CUP INJURY BY HANGING OUT WITH MEGAN MORONEY IN NASHVILLE

This is like an elite prospect getting called up from Triple-A. I'm talking the kind of prospect who's about to make 14 all-star teams, win an MVP and probably 10 Gold Gloves. There have been moments over my career where I just have a feeling that a content creator is going to be an Internet star.

Paulina Adams has all the intangibles. Now it's up to Sayin to go out and have a season. We could be looking at a Skenes-Dunne career trajectory if things work out this fall.

Trust me, Screencaps is BUCKLED UP.

KRISTIN CAVALLARI GETS RAVE REVIEWS FOR HER FIRST PITCH AT CUBS GAME, 7'6 15-YEAR-OLD BASKETBALL PLAYER & MEAT

📩 Email: joe.kinsey@outkick.com Send photos, stories, tips, rants—whatever you've got.

📰 Screencaps Page: 👉 Read the latest Screencaps

▶️ YouTube: Screencaps with Joe Kinsey Subscribe for videos, rants, and behind-the-scenes.

🐦 Twitter/X: @JoeKinseyexp Tag me or drop a DM.

📸 Instagram: @OutKickScreencaps You guys need to start tagging me on content you're seeing.

📘 Facebook Page: Screencaps on Facebook

👥 Facebook Group: Join the Screencaps Community

📬 Mail (Thursday Night Mowing League): 27072 Carronade Dr, Unit A 155 Perrysburg, OH 43551

🗞️ Newsletter: 👉 Subscribe here

After spending all summer acting, and dressing, like she was trying out for a ridiculous PBS Shakespeare TV series that your grandma fake-watched, Maria Sharapova is back to acting like the pop culture icon that we all fell in love with in the early 2000s.

I write about this quite a bit. I'm talking about the whole thing where women hit their late 30s and all of a sudden they're eating mushrooms, praying to butterfly gods and burning incense to cleanse the air in their apartment while a cat takes a dump in a litter trap placed in the kitchen.

There's nothing quite insufferable like these women going on podcasts and rambling about how they're reclaiming their souls or some stupid crap they've heard from some Oprah guest.

It's all a work, folks.

Then, when you're not looking, Maria and other ladies/moms in their late 30s are acting like they're in Fountain of Wayne's classic "Stacy's Mom" music video. Don't let them fool you with their Shakespearean Instagram posts.

The story of Shatner and his daughter popped up on our TV last night and I about came out of my recliner. The minute you don't see photos of a celebrity for several years is typically the time where they're in decline and near death. Then Shatner pops up on TV looking like he's 75.

This is a guy who has been married FOUR times. You'd think such a run would wear his ass out. Nope.

– Chris B. wants to take a shot at explaining things to his fellow readers: I don’t think the sale-leaseback is, in and of itself, a sign of impending doom for Cracker Barrel. The deal only includes 26/658 locations, or about 4%.

The technique isn’t unheard-of; I’ve seen lot of medical practice building sales for a cash infusion (doctors are notorious for post-divorce cash-flow problems).A lot of the success of the deal will depend on the new lease terms, since CB is exchanging equity for rent payments — so there will be a TBD effect on the P&L. Again, though, it’s a small number of properties.The same-store revenue drop year-over-year seems like it could be caused by decreased road travel, or changes to road tripping, because of costs for gas and every other damn thing.

If--and only if--CEO Masino does the right thing (TBD) and actually applies the $77MM to debt, the company might come out slightly stronger.All of this is speculative and subject to the usual disclaimers about things like hiring another woke moron to run marketing…

– Bill D. says doom could be on the horizon: All you really need to understand about what’s going on at Cracker Barrel is to look at the table on page 24 of their most recent Form 10-Q. This is the table where they show Cracker Barrel comparable store sales.

https://investor.crackerbarrel.com/static-files/8bc425e9-e009-4264-8d93-bf8d8dc0f958

Notice the disastrous numbers for the nine month FYTD period which show restaurant comparable sales down 4.9% yr/yr and retail (e.g. gift shop) sales down 6.9% yr/yr.

These are horribly bad numbers for a restaurant. As you wrote last September, the Cracker Barrel CEO embarked on a completely foolish attempt to rebrand the stores and the menu and it has been a disaster in terms of foot traffic to the restaurants and gift shops as well as comparable store sales. While the company claims to have reversed those rebranding decisions, the damage has been done as that table clearly shows.

The company will not go under tomorrow because they still have a lot of revenue coming in and they are still throwing off cash. But if they can’t stem the decline in same store sales and get customers coming in the door again then it will eventually fail.

Kinsey: DISCLAIMER: THE OPINIONS SHARED HERE ARE EXACTLY THAT, JUST OPINIONS AND OBSERVATIONS. DON'T THINK FOR A SECOND YOU CAN SUE ME, BILL D., CHRIS B., OR OUTKICK, BECAUSE I ADDED THIS DISCLAIMER THAT NOTHING WRITTEN IN THIS COLUMN SHOULD BE TAKEN AS FINANCIAL ADVICE. DUE YOUR OWN DUE DILIGENCE!

– Mike in Texas shares: Reading your column [Wednesday morning] and when I came across the article about Fort McCoy Wisconsin it brought back some memories. My Dad was stationed there in 1940, PRIOR to WWII! He was with the 5th (Red Diamond) Division, 21st Field Artillery, Battery B.

As a young, recently promoted 1st Lieutenant, he commanded that battery. Back then it was called Camp McCoy. Dad went on to serve 38 months overseas in Europe. The one episode that scarred him for life was his involvement in the liberation of Dachau at the end of April 1945.

– Dawgs fan Sam L. wants answers: With the continued onslaught of Cyclosporiasis, every article/headline labels the resulting 'explosive diarrhea'. My question is, who decides when such an event is 'explosive'?

Could one person's strong ejection equal another's explosive output?Thanks to South Park, we have a standard to measure turd sizes - The Couric.MSM is once again delivering fake news. How can I trust them that they know what 'explosive diarrhea' constitute? Have they investigated, found some parasites, and take one for the team to truly tell us if they suffered explosively?

'Cmon hack John Lemmon, ABC killjoy David Miur, CNN flunky Joe Tapper. Eat some Taco Bell with Lettuce and tells use if its a dribble, a flow, a splatter, a jet, or an explosion. Edward Murrow would have and spit tobacco juice in your faces, you sacks of lying non-explosive s–t.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

###################
